title: Recent developments in non-commutative Iwasawa theory - 1
speaker: David Burns

proof_stages.label: Equivariant Tamagawa-number framework
proof_stages.summary: The lecture starts from the Bloch-Kato Tamagawa number conjecture and explains how motives with extra endomorphisms lead to an equivariant, relative-K-theoretic refinement of the leading-term formula.

proof_stages.label: Noncommutative passage and Iwasawa motivation
proof_stages.summary: Fukaya-Kato's noncommutative Tamagawa-number conjecture is presented as an inverse-limit/adically-completed version of ETNC, motivating a noncommutative Iwasawa theory that can formulate equivariant main conjectures and twisting principles.

proof_stages.label: p-adic Lie extension and localized torsion hypothesis
proof_stages.summary: For a compact p-adic Lie Galois group G with distinguished quotient Gamma≅Z_p and kernel H, the speaker introduces the MH(G)-type finiteness condition and the Ore localizations S and S* of the Iwasawa algebra Lambda(G).

proof_stages.label: Perfect Selmer complex and refined Euler characteristic
proof_stages.summary: The relevant compactly supported or Selmer complex is perfect over Lambda(G) and becomes acyclic after S*-localization, so relative algebraic K-theory supplies a refined Euler characteristic generalizing the characteristic ideal.

proof_stages.label: Noncommutative p-adic L-function and main conjecture
proof_stages.summary: Artin representations of G define evaluation maps from K_1 of the localized Iwasawa algebra, and the noncommutative main conjecture asks for a K_1-class whose boundary is the refined Euler characteristic and whose evaluations interpolate leading L-values.

proof_stages.label: Descent to ETNC and the classical rank-one case
proof_stages.summary: The speaker compares the main conjecture with finite-level ETNC and Fukaya-Kato's noncommutative Tamagawa conjecture via descent, noting the required semisimplicity and torsion hypotheses and the reduction to the usual main conjecture in rank-one abelian cases.

proof_stages.label: K-theoretic reduction to rank-one abelian subquotients
proof_stages.summary: A map from K_1(Lambda(G) localized) to the product of total quotient rings for all rank-one abelian subquotients is introduced, and the main theorem says the noncommutative main conjecture is equivalent to the corresponding rank-one main conjectures plus compatibility congruences among their p-adic L-functions.

proof_stages.label: Consequences from congruences and applications
proof_stages.summary: Descriptions of the image/congruences, especially Kakde's methods and Ritter-Weiss rank-one results, make the reduction constructive and yield applications to totally real class-group Iwasawa theory and to zeta elements for varieties over finite fields.
